analysis.summary: Keycloak is an enterprise-grade identity and access management (IAM) server built on Quarkus that provides single sign-on (SSO), user federation, authentication, and authorization services. It implements OpenID Connect, OAuth 2.0, and SAML protocols for securing applications and services. The system follows a modular architecture with separate layers for authentication providers, user storage, policy engines, and protocol adapters.
capabilities: Processes OpenID Connect authentication flows including authorization code, implicit, and device flows (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/)
Implements SAML 2.0 identity provider and service provider functionality with metadata parsing (adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/)
Manages user authentication sessions with configurable timeouts and single sign-out (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/AuthenticationSessionManager.java)
Provides user federation with LDAP and Kerberos integration for external user stores (federation/ldap/, federation/kerberos/)
Implements fine-grained role-based access control (RBAC) with realm and client-level roles (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/RoleResource.java)
Executes JavaScript-based authorization policies with sandboxed evaluation (authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/js/)
Manages OAuth 2.0 scopes and consent screens with customizable user approval flows (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/OAuthGrantResource.java)
Processes multi-factor authentication with TOTP, WebAuthn, and SMS verification (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/)
Implements database-agnostic persistence layer with JPA/Hibernate and Infinispan caching (model/jpa/, model/infinispan/)
Provides REST API for administrative operations with comprehensive CRUD operations (rest/admin-ui-ext/, rest/admin-v2/)
Generates and validates JWT tokens with configurable signing algorithms and claims (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/TokenManager.java)
Implements user registration and password reset workflows with email verification (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/LoginActionsService.java)
Manages client certificates and key stores for mutual TLS authentication (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/x509/)
Executes authentication flows with configurable execution chains and conditions (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/)
Provides theme customization engine with FreeMarker templating for UI rendering (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/theme/)
Implements event logging and auditing with configurable event listeners (server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/events/)
Processes user profile management with custom attributes and validation (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/)
Manages realm configurations with hierarchical settings and inheritance (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/RealmAdminResource.java)
Implements protocol mappers for token customization and claim transformation (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/ProtocolMapper.java)
Provides client adapter libraries for Java applications with SAML and OpenID Connect support (adapters/)
Executes database migrations with Liquibase for schema versioning (model/jpa/src/main/resources/META-INF/jpa-changelog-master.xml)
Implements clustering support with Infinispan distributed caching for high availability (model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/cluster/infinispan/)
Processes SCIM 2.0 protocol for user provisioning and synchronization (scim/)
Provides operator for Kubernetes deployments with custom resource definitions (operator/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/)
Implements WebAuthn/FIDO2 authentication with passkey support (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/browser/WebAuthnAuthenticator.java)
use_cases: Enterprise IT teams use Keycloak to implement single sign-on across web applications and microservices with centralized user management
DevOps engineers deploy Keycloak as an identity provider for securing APIs and services with OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect
Application developers integrate Keycloak adapters to add authentication and authorization to Java web applications
Security architects implement zero-trust security models using Keycloak's fine-grained authorization policies and multi-factor authentication
Platform teams use the Keycloak Operator to manage identity services in Kubernetes environments with automated scaling and updates
analysis.target_audience: Enterprise architects, DevOps engineers, Java developers, Security engineers, Platform engineers
api_endpoints: GET /admin/realms/{realm}/users — Admin API for user management
POST /admin/realms/{realm}/users — Create new user via admin API
auth_methods: OpenID Connect with authorization code, implicit, and device flows (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/)
OAuth 2.0 with client credentials and refresh token flows (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/)
SAML 2.0 identity provider and service provider (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/saml/)
Multi-factor authentication with TOTP, WebAuthn, and SMS (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/)
JWT bearer token authentication with configurable signing algorithms (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/TokenManager.java)
Role-based access control (RBAC) with realm and client roles (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/RoleResource.java)
integrations: LDAP and Active Directory for user federation (federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/)
Kerberos for single sign-on in Windows environments (federation/kerberos/)
PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, Oracle databases via JPA/Hibernate (model/jpa/)
Infinispan for distributed caching and session replication (model/infinispan/)
SMTP email servers for user verification and notifications (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/email/)
Social identity providers (Google, Facebook, GitHub, etc.) via OpenID Connect (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/)
SCIM 2.0 for user provisioning and synchronization (scim/)
Kubernetes via custom operator for cloud-native deployments (operator/)
Vault for secret management and key storage (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/vault/)
Prometheus for metrics and monitoring (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/UserSessionManager.java)

components: Authentication Services - handles login flows and multi-factor authentication (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/)
Protocol Handlers - implements OIDC, OAuth 2.0, and SAML protocols (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/)
User Storage SPI - provides pluggable user federation (server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/)
Authorization Engine - evaluates policies and permissions (authz/)
Account Console - React-based user self-service UI (js/apps/account-ui/)
Event System - auditing and logging framework (server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/events/)
architecture.data_flow: HTTP requests flow through JAX-RS resources to service managers, which interact with the storage layer (JPA/Infinispan). Authentication flows execute through configurable execution chains, while authorization decisions are evaluated by the policy engine. Session data is cached in Infinispan for performance and distributed across cluster nodes.
architecture.persistence: JPA/Hibernate for relational data with Liquibase migrations, Infinispan for caching and session storage, supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, Oracle, H2
architecture.concurrency_model: async with Quarkus reactive extensions and traditional thread-per-request for JAX-RS endpoints
architecture.communication_patterns: HTTP REST APIs for admin and user interfaces, OpenID Connect/OAuth 2.0/SAML protocols for client communication, JPA for database access, Infinispan for inter-node communication
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Service Provider Interface (SPI) pattern allows custom authentication flows, user storage providers, protocol mappers, event listeners, and themes

primary_entities: Realm - security domain containing users, roles, and clients
User - identity with credentials and attributes
Client - application or service requiring authentication
Role - permission assignment for users and clients
Group - user organization with hierarchical roles
storage_backends: Infinispan for distributed caching and sessions
File system for themes and configurations
data_model.schema_patterns: Relational model with JPA entities, normalized tables for users/roles/clients, denormalized session cache, hierarchical groups and policies
data_model.migration_strategy: Liquibase changesets for database schema evolution with automatic migration on startup (model/jpa/src/main/resources/META-INF/jpa-changelog-master.xml)

algorithms.problem_type: Identity and access management with secure authentication, authorization policy evaluation, and distributed session management
core_patterns: Chain of Responsibility for authentication flows
Strategy pattern for user storage providers and protocol handlers
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Complex policy evaluation engine with JavaScript sandboxing, distributed session management across cluster nodes, protocol conversion between OIDC/OAuth2/SAML
key_algorithms: JWT signing and validation with RSA/ECDSA algorithms
OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with PKCE
Policy evaluation with JavaScript engine isolation
algorithms.evidence: services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/TokenManager.java for JWT algorithms, services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/ for authentication flows, authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/js/ for policy evaluation

security_model.authentication: Multi-factor authentication with TOTP, WebAuthn, SMS; social login providers; certificate-based authentication; Kerberos/SPNEGO
security_model.authorization: Fine-grained RBAC with realm and client roles, policy-based authorization with JavaScript rules, resource and scope-based permissions
security_model.data_protection: JWT token signing with configurable algorithms, TLS/SSL for transport encryption, secure session management with httpOnly cookies
security_model.security_headers: Configurable CORS policies, CSP headers, secure cookie attributes, rate limiting on authentication endpoints
security_model.vulnerability_handling: SECURITY.md file present, dependency scanning in CI/CD, regular security updates and CVE tracking

deployment.container_orchestration: Docker and Kubernetes with custom operator
deployment.environment_config: Environment variables, system properties, configuration files, and database-stored realm settings
deployment.scaling_model: Horizontal scaling with Infinispan clustering for session replication and load balancing

code_quality_signals.code_style: Maven Checkstyle, ESLint for JavaScript/TypeScript, Prettier formatting

project_health.top_contributors: Red Hat employees and community contributors
project_health.recent_activity: Active development with regular releases, security updates, and feature additions
project_health.community_signals: CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md, issue templates, governance documentation

information_gaps: Exact performance benchmarks and scalability limits
Complete list of supported social identity providers

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: The system processes authentication flows with configurable execution chains, validates JWT tokens with signing algorithms, and executes JavaScript-based authorization policies with sandboxed evaluation. It implements multiple authentication protocols (OIDC, SAML, OAuth 2.0) and manages user sessions with configurable timeouts.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Evidence: Strict JPA entity models, OpenID Connect/SAML protocol compliance, REST API with comprehensive CRUD operations, and Liquibase schema migrations indicate rigid data contracts and protocol adherence.
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Evidence from authentication flow execution chains with configurable conditions, session state management with timeouts and transitions, and policy evaluation engines that process rules through defined states and transitions.
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Evidence from Infinispan distributed caching for session replication across cluster nodes, JPA/Hibernate for database persistence, and Quarkus reactive extensions supporting both async and traditional threading models.
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Maintains identity verification state and access permissions across distributed clients through protocol-agnostic token exchange with configurable policy evaluation and session lifecycle management.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: Request → protocol validation → authentication flow execution → policy evaluation → token generation → session persistence → response with distributed cache synchronization
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The core SPI pattern and pluggable architecture make many components highly reusable. Authentication flows, session management, and policy engines are abstract enough for other domains. However, protocol handlers (OIDC, SAML) and user federation components are identity-specific. The distributed caching, REST API framework, and plugin system could be repurposed for any system requiring secure state management across multiple clients.

evidence_claims.claim: Implements configurable authentication flow execution chains
Uses Service Provider Interface pattern for extensibility
Employs distributed caching with Infinispan for session replication
Executes JavaScript-based policies in sandboxed environment
Built on Quarkus runtime with reactive extensions
evidence_claims.evidence: quarkus/runtime/pom.xml and async concurrency model documentation

cross_industry_applications: Healthcare Systems - Patient record access control with HIPAA compliance and audit trails
Financial Services - Multi-factor authentication for banking applications with fraud detection policies
Manufacturing IoT - Device authentication and authorization for industrial control systems
Educational Platforms - Student/faculty access management across learning management systems
Government/Public Sector - Citizen portal access with various authentication methods and security clearance levels
Gaming/Entertainment - Player authentication with social login integration and content access control
Supply Chain Management - Vendor/partner access control with role-based permissions across logistics systems
Telecommunications - Subscriber authentication and service authorization for network access control

friction_report.migration_path: Extract core authentication flow engine and session management components, replace protocol handlers with domain-specific adapters, implement custom user storage providers, and adapt the admin UI for specific use case requirements.

comparable_systems: Istio Service Mesh - Policy enforcement and request routing with pluggable architecture
Kong API Gateway - Request processing pipelines with plugin extensibility and distributed configuration
HashiCorp Vault - Secret management with policy engines and multiple authentication backends
Apache Kafka Connect - Data pipeline orchestration with pluggable connectors and distributed coordination
Kubernetes RBAC - Resource access control with role-based policies and distributed state management
information_gaps: Exact scalability limits and performance benchmarks under load
Complete list of supported social identity providers and their configuration
Detailed FIPS compliance features and cryptographic implementations
Specific clustering topology requirements and network configurations
